Turn 17 ‚Äî degree 20 dies; the residual tower now has an induction shape I continued from the three files attached to this turn. The original target remains the over-G isomorphism question for ŒìS,ŒìH, where the problem statement defines F=P‚ãäG, the relators rS,rH, and the condition œÄH‚àòŒ¶=œÄS.

Turn 16 ended with the residual mod-2, s=1, Ef-piece solved throughS(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI20),and identified the next possible obstruction asQ20:=L20/im‚àÇ19‚âÖL10[2],dimL10=105.It also warned correctly that degree 20 is not just another odd-root transgression, because L10 is even and already contains a square quotient L5[2].

The new result is:The¬Ýdegree-20¬Ýresidual¬Ýobstruction¬Ýdies.

More precisely, the residual congruence can be improved toS(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI21),and then degree 21 clears by the odd-layer argument, givingS(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI22).The useful object is not a fixed degree-20 tail. Define Cn‚äÜLn to be the space of degree-n primitive tails that can be introduced as temporary residual tails and then continued through the already-cleared obstruction tower. Turn 15 introduced this viewpoint for C9, and Turn 16 provedC9=L9,which killed degree 18 by the transgression W‚Ü¶W[2].

For degree 20, the corresponding test is:C10=?L10.

This time L10 is not automatically an ordinary boundary, but it has the standard restricted decompositionL10/im‚àÇ9‚âÖL5[2].

The boundary part is easy:im‚àÇ9‚äÜC10,because degree-9 corrections on Y1 and q introduce tails of the form‚àÇ9 (U,V)=[A,U]+[B,V].The non-boundary part is exactly the six-dimensional square quotient L5[2]. But this was already the degree-10 square mechanism from Turn 7: the degree-8 kernel freedom maps ontoL10/im‚àÇ9‚âÖL5[2].

In Turn 7‚Äôs notation,ker‚àÇ8‚ÜÝL5[2].

So every square component of L10 is also continuably introducible. Combining the ordinary boundary part with this square part givesC10=L10.This is the structural close of degree 20. SinceQ20‚âÖL10[2],the transgressionT10:C10‚ÜíQ20,W‚Ü¶W[2],is onto. Modulo im‚àÇ19, it is linear, because in characteristic 2,(W+W‚Ä≤)[2]=W[2]+W‚Ä≤[2]+[W,W‚Ä≤],and the cross-bracket [W,W‚Ä≤]‚ààL20 is ordinary Lie material, hence dies in the square quotient Q20.Thus any actual degree-20 square class can be shifted into im‚àÇ19, and then killed by a degree-19 correction. HenceS(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI21).Now degree 21 is odd. As in the Turn 6 odd-layer mechanism,L21=[A,L20]+[B,L20],so any first degree-21 primitive tail is an ordinary boundary. A degree-20 correction kills it, givingS(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI22).The dimensions are consistent with this picture. For the free restricted Lie algebra on A,B, the Hilbert series identity 1‚àí2t1=n‚â•1‚àè(1+tn)‚Ñìn,‚Ñìn:=dimLn,gives‚Ñì10=105,‚Ñì19=27594,‚Ñì20=52482.Thus rank‚àÇ19=52482‚àí105=52377,and the cokernel has exactly the expected 105-dimensional square quotient L10[2].The larger structural point is now clearer. The relevant pattern is:Cm=Lm‚üπQ2m=L2m/im‚àÇ2m‚àí1‚âÖLm[2]is¬Ýfully¬Ýhit¬Ýby¬ÝW‚Ü¶W[2].

So once Cm=Lm, degree 2m dies, and degree 2m+1 dies formally because it is odd.

Turn 16 proved this for m=9, killing degrees 18 and 19. The argument above proves it for m=10, killing degrees 20 and 21. The next layer, degree 22, has root L11, and 11 is odd, so the same boundary-root mechanism should apply there: the next genuinely new even-root test will not be degree 22, but the next even-root layer where one must again account for restricted-square root material.This still does not by itself finish the original over-G isomorphism problem. What it does establish is that the strongest obstruction tower found so far continues to collapse for structural reasons rather than by one-off tail cancellations.

ESTABLISHED: C10=L10. Therefore the degree-20 square quotientL20/im‚àÇ19‚âÖL10[2]is fully hit by the transgression W‚Ü¶W[2], so degree 20 clears. Degree 21 clears by the odd-layer boundary argument. The residual congruence is solvable throughS(Y0,Y1,q)‚â°X2 (modI22).

GAP: This is still not yet a complete proof of ŒìS‚âÖGŒìH. The residual obstruction tower now has a strong inductive pattern, but the remaining work is to formalize it as a compatible inverse-limit construction and then lift/glue it back from the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1 piece to an actual continuous G-operator Nielsen automorphism, with the section cocycle included.

NEXT: Turn the observed pattern into a precise induction: prove that Cm=Lm for all sufficiently large m, using the recursionLm/im‚àÇm‚àí1‚âÖ{0,Lm/2[2],m¬Ýodd,m¬Ýeven,together with the already-established base block through m=10. If this induction is made rigorous, the residual Magnus obstruction tower vanishes to all orders.[DEG20-C10-FULL-DEG21-CLEARED]
